steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to compare the hardness of talc and topaz specifically. A successful choice must mention both minerals and use the Mohs principle (scratching ability) to state which is harder.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Topaz can leave visible scratches on fluorite, which is why topaz has a higher number than fluorite on the Mohs scale of mineral hardness.': Compares topaz and fluorite, not topaz and talc. Neither talc nor the talc-topaz comparison appears. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The Mohs scale of mineral hardness can be used to order fluorite, talc, and topaz by their ability to scratch other minerals.': Mentions all three minerals but makes no direct comparison between talc and topaz — it only says the scale can order them. The hardness comparison the student needs is absent. ✗
  • Choice C — 'A mineral with a Mohs number of 4, like fluorite, is harder than one with a Mohs number of 1, like talc.': Compares fluorite and talc, not topaz and talc. Topaz is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Topaz can leave visible scratches on talc, which means that topaz is harder than talc.': Directly names both target minerals, applies the Mohs scratching principle, and draws the hardness conclusion. This is the only choice that does all three. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
